What is machine learning and how does it work?

Machine learning (ML) is a branch of artificial intelligence where systems learn from data to improve their performance without being explicitly programmed.

How it works

  • Training — the model learns patterns from labeled examples
  • Inference — the trained model makes predictions on new data
  • Evaluation — performance is measured on a held-out test set
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
predictions = model.predict(X_test)

What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?

Supervised learning uses labeled training data — you provide input-output pairs and the model learns the mapping. Common examples: image classification, spam detection.

Unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns in unlabeled data without any guidance. Common examples: clustering, dimensionality reduction, anomaly detection.
